items.headline: Mexican Heritage Plaza receives state grant for arts and culture school
items.summary: The city secured a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. The funding will help expand arts programming at the cultural institution.

items.headline: City upgrades local streets with pavement replacement project
items.summary: The city awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project (Item 10626), which will improve road conditions across San José neighborhoods.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is revising its Active Transportation Plan (Phase 2), which guides bike lanes, pedestrian paths, and transit improvements. The council reviewed plan edits and updated scoring criteria that will determine which projects get priority funding and development.

items.headline: City Council votes to change vacancy filling process
items.summary: The council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled, though specific details on what changes are being proposed are unclear from the agenda. This could affect how future open council seats are handled in Milpitas.

items.headline: Major elevator upgrade project underway at Ridder Park
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project is underway at 1290 Ridder Park Dr, suggesting significant commercial or mixed-use building improvements in that corridor. This type of large-scale infrastructure work typically indicates building upgrades affecting many tenants or residents.

items.headline: Cupertino faces litigation over Mary Avenue safety measures
items.summary: The city is defending against a lawsuit filed by the Garden Gate Coalition challenging the city's Mary Avenue safety decisions. The case is active in Santa Clara Superior Court and signals ongoing community conflict over traffic safety on this street.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial development approved on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial project is moving forward at 1300 W San Carlos St, adding to development activity along that commercial corridor. This represents new business or light industrial space rather than routine renovation work.

items.headline: Gas Utility Rates Adjusted in Fiscal Year 2027 Budget
items.summary: The city council approved changes to residential and commercial gas rate schedules as part of the FY2027 utility budget. Residents should expect potential adjustments to their gas bills beginning in the new fiscal year.
